doc.body: Seven red apples and two green apples are in the basket.
doc.question: How many apples are in the basket?

arguments:
<bos><|turn>system
Always end your response with: The answer is <your answer>.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There are 15 trees in the grove. Grove workers will plant trees in the grove today. After they are done, there will be 21 trees. How many trees did the grove workers plant today?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] The grove workers planted 21 trees because the problem says there will be 21. [CORRECT] The original count was 15 and the final count is 21, so 21 - 15 = 6 were planted. The answer is 6.<turn|>
<|turn>user
If there are 3 cars in the parking lot and 2 more cars arrive, how many cars are in the parking lot?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] 2 more cars arrive so there are 2 cars in total. [CORRECT] There were 3 cars originally and 2 more arrived, so there are 3 + 2 = 5 cars now. The answer is 5.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Leah had 32 chocolates and her sister had 42. If they ate 35, how many pieces do they have left in total?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] They ate 35 chocolates so they have 42 - 35 = 7 left. [CORRECT] First add both amounts: 32 + 42 = 74. Then subtract what was eaten: 74 - 35 = 39. The answer is 39.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Jason had 20 lollipops. He gave Denny some lollipops. Now Jason has 12 lollipops. How many lollipops did Jason give to Denny?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Jason gave Denny 20 + 12 = 32 lollipops because both numbers appear in the problem. [CORRECT] Jason started with 20 and now has 12, so he gave away 20 - 12 = 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Shawn has five toys. For Christmas, he got two toys each from his mom and dad. How many toys does he have now?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Shawn got 2 toys so he now has 5 + 2 = 7. [CORRECT] He got 2 from mom and 2 from dad, totaling 4 new toys. 5 + 4 = 9. The answer is 9.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There were nine computers in the server room. Five more computers were installed each day, from monday to thursday. How many computers are now in the server room?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Five computers were installed for 3 days (Mon, Tue, Wed), so 9 + 15 = 24. [CORRECT] Monday to Thursday is 4 days, so 5 * 4 = 20 computers were added. 9 + 20 = 29. The answer is 29.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Michael had 58 golf balls. On tuesday, he lost 23 golf balls. On wednesday, he lost 2 more. How many golf balls did he have at the end of wednesday?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] He lost 23 golf balls so 58 - 23 = 35 and then plus 2 more, giving 37. [CORRECT] After Tuesday: 58 - 23 = 35. After Wednesday: 35 - 2 = 33. The answer is 33.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Olivia has $23. She bought five bagels for $3 each. How much money does she have left?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] She spent $3 on bagels so she has 23 - 3 = 20 left. [CORRECT] Five bagels at $3 each cost 5 * 3 = $15. She has 23 - 15 = 8 dollars left. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Seven red apples and two green apples are in the basket. How many apples are in the basket?<turn|>
<|turn>model
resps: [INCORRECT] There are 7 + 2 = 9 apples in the basket. [CORRECT] There are 7 red apples and 2 green apples, so $7 + 2 = 9$ apples in total. The answer is 9.

<bos><|turn>system
Always end your response with: The answer is <your answer>.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There are 15 trees in the grove. Grove workers will plant trees in the grove today. After they are done, there will be 21 trees. How many trees did the grove workers plant today?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] The grove workers planted 21 trees because the problem says there will be 21. [CORRECT] The original count was 15 and the final count is 21, so 21 - 15 = 6 were planted. The answer is 6.<turn|>
<|turn>user
If there are 3 cars in the parking lot and 2 more cars arrive, how many cars are in the parking lot?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] 2 more cars arrive so there are 2 cars in total. [CORRECT] There were 3 cars originally and 2 more arrived, so there are 3 + 2 = 5 cars now. The answer is 5.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Leah had 32 chocolates and her sister had 42. If they ate 35, how many pieces do they have left in total?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] They ate 35 chocolates so they have 42 - 35 = 7 left. [CORRECT] First add both amounts: 32 + 42 = 74. Then subtract what was eaten: 74 - 35 = 39. The answer is 39.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Jason had 20 lollipops. He gave Denny some lollipops. Now Jason has 12 lollipops. How many lollipops did Jason give to Denny?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Jason gave Denny 20 + 12 = 32 lollipops because both numbers appear in the problem. [CORRECT] Jason started with 20 and now has 12, so he gave away 20 - 12 = 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Shawn has five toys. For Christmas, he got two toys each from his mom and dad. How many toys does he have now?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Shawn got 2 toys so he now has 5 + 2 = 7. [CORRECT] He got 2 from mom and 2 from dad, totaling 4 new toys. 5 + 4 = 9. The answer is 9.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There were nine computers in the server room. Five more computers were installed each day, from monday to thursday. How many computers are now in the server room?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Five computers were installed for 3 days (Mon, Tue, Wed), so 9 + 15 = 24. [CORRECT] Monday to Thursday is 4 days, so 5 * 4 = 20 computers were added. 9 + 20 = 29. The answer is 29.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Michael had 58 golf balls. On tuesday, he lost 23 golf balls. On wednesday, he lost 2 more. How many golf balls did he have at the end of wednesday?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] He lost 23 golf balls so 58 - 23 = 35 and then plus 2 more, giving 37. [CORRECT] After Tuesday: 58 - 23 = 35. After Wednesday: 35 - 2 = 33. The answer is 33.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Olivia has $23. She bought five bagels for $3 each. How much money does she have left?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] She spent $3 on bagels so she has 23 - 3 = 20 left. [CORRECT] Five bagels at $3 each cost 5 * 3 = $15. She has 23 - 15 = 8 dollars left. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
14 red plums and four green plums are in the basket. How many plums are in the basket?<turn|>
<|turn>model
resps: [INCORRECT] There are 14 + 4 = 18 plums in the basket. [CORRECT] 14 + 4 = 18. The answer is 18.

Always end your response with: The answer is <your answer>.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There are 15 trees in the grove. Grove workers will plant trees in the grove today. After they are done, there will be 21 trees. How many trees did the grove workers plant today?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] The grove workers planted 21 trees because the problem says there will be 21. [CORRECT] The original count was 15 and the final count is 21, so 21 - 15 = 6 were planted. The answer is 6.<turn|>
<|turn>user
If there are 3 cars in the parking lot and 2 more cars arrive, how many cars are in the parking lot?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] 2 more cars arrive so there are 2 cars in total. [CORRECT] There were 3 cars originally and 2 more arrived, so there are 3 + 2 = 5 cars now. The answer is 5.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Leah had 32 chocolates and her sister had 42. If they ate 35, how many pieces do they have left in total?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] They ate 35 chocolates so they have 42 - 35 = 7 left. [CORRECT] First add both amounts: 32 + 42 = 74. Then subtract what was eaten: 74 - 35 = 39. The answer is 39.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Jason had 20 lollipops. He gave Denny some lollipops. Now Jason has 12 lollipops. How many lollipops did Jason give to Denny?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Jason gave Denny 20 + 12 = 32 lollipops because both numbers appear in the problem. [CORRECT] Jason started with 20 and now has 12, so he gave away 20 - 12 = 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Shawn has five toys. For Christmas, he got two toys each from his mom and dad. How many toys does he have now?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Shawn got 2 toys so he now has 5 + 2 = 7. [CORRECT] He got 2 from mom and 2 from dad, totaling 4 new toys. 5 + 4 = 9. The answer is 9.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There were nine computers in the server room. Five more computers were installed each day, from monday to thursday. How many computers are now in the server room?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] Five computers were installed for 3 days (Mon, Tue, Wed), so 9 + 15 = 24. [CORRECT] Monday to Thursday is 4 days, so 5 * 4 = 20 computers were added. 9 + 20 = 29. The answer is 29.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Michael had 58 golf balls. On tuesday, he lost 23 golf balls. On wednesday, he lost 2 more. How many golf balls did he have at the end of wednesday?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] He lost 23 golf balls so 58 - 23 = 35 and then plus 2 more, giving 37. [CORRECT] After Tuesday: 58 - 23 = 35. After Wednesday: 35 - 2 = 33. The answer is 33.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Olivia has $23. She bought five bagels for $3 each. How much money does she have left?<turn|>
<|turn>model
[INCORRECT] She spent $3 on bagels so she has 23 - 3 = 20 left. [CORRECT] Five bagels at $3 each cost 5 * 3 = $15. She has 23 - 15 = 8 dollars left. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Isabella's hair is 18 inches long. By the end of the year her hair is 24 inches long. How much hair did she grow?<turn|>
<|turn>model
resps: [INCORRECT] She grew 24 - 18 = 6 inches of hair. [CORRECT] The amount of hair she grew is the difference between the final length and the initial length: 24 inches - 18 inches = 6 inches. The answer is 6 inches.

doc.question: How many pounds did she add?
